_comment: NodeKit showcase deck. Compiled by the SAME scripts/build-atlas-v3-native.mjs as the Atlas museum deck and graded by the same parity gates — a second builder would be a second set of behaviours to keep honest. Every payload below is a number or a name verified against a real file in this repo or in parity-studio during the 2026-07-23 session; nothing is illustrative. Slides a viewer would expect but that have no material behind them are recorded in refused.json rather than invented.
communicationJob: Show that a slide is finished only when the artifact it claims is actually present in the bytes — and that this deck was built and graded by that rule.

fixtures.title: Every slide here was gated by the rule it describes
fixtures.takeaway: 53 archetypes, each naming the artifact that must exist on the slide and the near-misses that do not count.

payload.label: 53 archetypes, each naming the artifact that must exist on the slide and the near-misses that do not count.

fixtures.title: Six artifact kinds are visible to a shallow reader, ten to a deep one
fixtures.takeaway: One kind — scrollytelling — can never be observed in PowerPoint at all.

fixtures._comment: Deliberately NOT progression.before-after: that archetype is asset-routed in the compiler and would replace this chart with a workspace screenshot, which does not show the claim the title makes.
fixtures.title: Zero native chart parts became ten
fixtures.takeaway: The v3 museum deck carried 1,303 shapes and not one native chart; the native rebuild carries ten real chart parts.

fixtures.title: The gate decides every slide, or says it could not
fixtures.takeaway: 36 passes, 2 declared fallbacks, zero unknowns — 100% of slides decided.

fixtures.title: The model's authority ends at the plan
fixtures.takeaway: Everything after the plan is decided by reading the emitted bytes, never the model's own claim.

fixtures.title: Portability is recorded per artifact kind, and measured twice
fixtures.takeaway: Charts, tables, diagrams and equations all survive LibreOffice; only the chart-to-workbook link does not.

fixtures.title: The equation is a real object, and it took a wrapper to make it travel
fixtures.takeaway: A bare m:oMath is well-formed, invisible, and deleted outright by LibreOffice.

fixtures.title: Eighty-two of eighty-four candidates cleared every gate
fixtures.takeaway: 72 of those receipts came from real models with paid telemetry; 12 from the deterministic baseline.

fixtures.title: Maturity is derived from receipts, never written beside them
fixtures.takeaway: Every recipe stops at proven, because the top rung needs a human and no human review has run.

fixtures.title: One gate is open, and no amount of compute can close it
fixtures.takeaway: Certified requires a human blind preference; humanPreferred is null on all 84 receipts.

payload.label: Certified requires a human blind preference. humanPreferred is null on all 84 receipts, so the ladder stops at proven and says so on the surface.
